title: Reduce BGP Scanner CPU Hog - Optimize BGP Timers
description: BGP Scanner process consumes excessive CPU cycles. Optimize BGP timers to reduce scanning frequency and CPU load on control plane.
steps: Access device CLI: ssh admin@device_ip
Enter configuration mode: configure terminal
Navigate to BGP config: router bgp ASN
Reduce BGP scan interval: timers bgp keepalive 15 holdtime 45
Reduce update group refresh: bgp additional-paths advertise-set best 16
Limit neighbor count per task: bgp maxas-limit 1000
Apply changes: end
Verify CPU reduction: show process cpu sorted | include BGP
reranker_text: BGP Scanner process causing high CPU utilization. Solution: optimize BGP timers to reduce scanning frequency. Configure keepalive 15 and holdtime 45.

title: Clear BGP Cache and Reduce Memory Pressure
description: BGP cache accumulation causes high memory utilization. Clearing BGP adjacency cache and RIB optimizes memory usage.
steps: Access device CLI: ssh admin@device_ip
Check current memory: show memory sorted
Check BGP RIB size: show bgp summary | include prefixes
Flush BGP adjacency: clear bgp * all soft (use with caution)
Soft-clear is preferred: clear bgp ipv4 * soft
Monitor memory after clear: show memory sorted
Configure BGP soft-configuration limit: neighbor * soft-reconfiguration inbound
Verify improvement: show memory | include Processor
reranker_text: High memory utilization from BGP cache. Solution: clear BGP adjacency cache using soft-clear commands to reduce memory pressure.

title: Emergency Fan Speed Increase - Address Thermal Threshold Exceeded
description: Temperature threshold exceeded. Immediately increase fan speed to cool down device. If fans fail to bring temperature down, device shutdown may occur.
steps: Check current temperature: show environment temperature
Check fan status: show environment fan
Increase fan speed: hw-module subslot slot_number fan speed auto
Monitor temperature: show environment temperature | include Temperature
Check if cooling ducts are blocked - verify airflow
If temperature does not drop in 5 minutes, prepare for shutdown
Consider device relocation if in hot environment
Schedule maintenance: check thermal paste, ensure proper mounting
Monitor continuously: show environment temperature repeat 5
reranker_text: Critical temperature threshold exceeded. Emergency action: increase fan speed to maximum using hw-module fan speed auto command.

title: Reduce Processing Load - Mitigation for Thermal Stress
description: Device experiencing thermal stress. Reduce processing load by disabling non-critical features and services.
steps: Disable non-critical process monitoring: no process max-fd watchdog
Reduce netflow export interval: flow-sampler-map SFLOW sampler DATA sample 1 out-of 1000
Disable unused features: no ip cef accounting
Disable SNMP if not critical: no snmp-server enable traps
Reduce logging verbosity: logging level syslog 6
Disable unused protocols: no ip igmp snooping
Monitor temperature: show environment temperature
Enable back services once temperature normalizes
reranker_text: Thermal stress mitigation: disable non-critical features and reduce processing load to lower device temperature.

title: Check and Reseat Transceiver - Link Down Remedy
description: Interface down. Check transceiver module seating and optical cable connections. Reseat if needed.
steps: Check interface status: show interface | include up/down
Check transceiver status: show inventory | include SFP
Check transceiver temperature: show interfaces status | include status
Power down interface: interface Te0/0/0
Shutdown interface: shutdown
Wait 10 seconds
Remove transceiver module gently
Inspect for dust/dirt - clean if needed
Reseat transceiver firmly until click
No shutdown interface: no shutdown
Verify link is up: show interface Te0/0/0 | include protocol
Check error counters: show interface Te0/0/0 statistics
If still down, replace transceiver
reranker_text: Interface down or line protocol down. Solution: check and reseat optical transceiver module to restore connectivity.

title: Check Optical Cable - Resolve Link Quality Issues
description: Link experiencing high error rates or intermittent flaps. Check optical cable for damage, kinks, or misalignment.
steps: Check interface error counters: show interface Te0/0/0 | include errors
Check optical power levels: show interfaces transceiver
Expected RX power: -30 to -10 dBm for long haul, -12 to 0 dBm for short
If RX power too low, check cable insertion
Trace cable path for visible damage (bends, crushing, light)
Visually inspect connector ends with magnifier
Check for dust on connectors using 1-click cleaner
Try replacing fiber optic cable
If power levels normal but errors persist, replace transceiver
Monitor CRC: show interface Te0/0/0 | include CRC
reranker_text: Link quality issues: check optical cable for damage or improper insertion. Verify optical power levels match specifications.

title: Perform BGP Soft Reset - Restore BGP Adjacency
description: BGP neighbor session unstable or flapping. Perform soft reset to gracefully clear BGP adjacency without full reload.
steps: Verify BGP status: show bgp summary
Identify problematic neighbor IP: show bgp ipv4 neighbors | include Neighbor
Perform inbound soft reset: clear bgp 10.0.0.1 soft in
Perform outbound soft reset: clear bgp 10.0.0.1 soft out
Wait 30 seconds for adjacency to re-establish
Verify BGP session: show bgp ipv4 neighbors 10.0.0.1 | include state
Check prefix count: show bgp ipv4 summary | include prefixes
If session remains unstable, check configuration: show run | include bgp
Verify neighbor IP reachability: ping 10.0.0.1
reranker_text: BGP neighbor session unstable. Solution: use soft reset (clear bgp X.X.X.X soft) to gracefully re-establish adjacency without reload.

title: Configure Route Redistribution Limits - Prevent Route Explosion
description: Excessive route redistribution causing routing table explosion and memory pressure. Limit prefix counts per source.
steps: Check current route count: show ip route summary | include Total
Check BGP RIB: show bgp summary | include prefixes
Enter BGP config: router bgp 65000
Set maximum prefix limit: maximum-prefix 100000 80 restart 300
Set per-neighbor limit: neighbor 10.0.0.1 maximum-prefix 50000 90 restart 300
Configure warning threshold: bgp maxas-limit 1000
Set slow-peer detection: bgp scan-time 15
Apply config: end
Monitor route growth: show ip route summary | include Total
Check if limit triggered: show bgp scan-time
reranker_text: Route explosion from excessive redistribution. Solution: configure maximum-prefix limits in BGP to prevent unbounded route table growth.

title: Perform Controlled Device Reload - System Recovery
description: Device experiencing critical system failure or recovery needed. Perform controlled reload to restore to known good state.
steps: Notify affected stakeholders of planned maintenance
Save configuration: copy run start
Verify saved config: show start | include version
Schedule reload for off-peak hours: reload at 02:00
Or reload immediately: reload
Confirm reload: yes
Monitor device boot via out-of-band console
Post-reload, verify configs: show run | include !
Verify all interfaces: show interface brief
Verify routing status: show ip route summary
Check system logs: show log | last 20
reranker_text: Critical system failure. Solution: perform controlled device reload to restore device to known good state from stored configuration.

title: Review and Adjust ACL Rules - Resolve Authentication Failures
description: Authentication or access failures due to ACL rules blocking legitimate traffic. Review and adjust access control lists.
steps: Check current ACLs: show access-lists
Verify source IP addresses: show ip source binding
Check inbound ACL on interface: show run interface Te0/0/0 | include access-group
Analyze ACL hits: show access-lists | include permit deny
Check deny counters: show access-lists detailed | include deny
Identify blocked traffic: debug ip packet detail
Run for 30 seconds then disable: undebug all
Review and modify ACL: ip access-list extended ALLOW_NMS
Add required source: permit ip 10.0.0.0 0.0.0.255 any
Verify ACL applied: show ip access-lists ALLOW_NMS
Re-test authentication: test aaa authentication admin USER PASSWORD
Monitor with: debug ip packet detail (verify passing now)
reranker_text: Authentication failure due to ACL restrictions. Solution: review and adjust ACL rules to permit legitimate traffic sources.

title: Emergency Shutdown and Safe Harbor Procedure
description: Device in critical state with multiple cascading failures. Execute emergency shutdown to preserve data and prevent further damage.
steps: Alert all stakeholders immediately
SAVE CONFIGURATION FIRST: copy run start
Backup running config to TFTP: copy run tftp://10.0.0.1/backup_config
Verify backup: show file tftp://10.0.0.1/backup_config | head
Gracefully disable services:
- Disable SNMP traps: no snmp-server enable traps all
- Disable logging: no logging console
- Disable CDP/LLDP: no cdp run
Clear BGP neighbors gracefully: clear bgp * graceful-restart
Initiate controlled shutdown: shutdown
Wait 30 seconds for graceful shutdown
If hung, access out-of-band: ssh to console server
Power cycle device if necessary
Do NOT force power off without saving configuration first
reranker_text: Emergency: device in critical cascading failure state. Solution: execute safe harbor procedure with configuration save before shutdown.
